name: Florence (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the silver field of the city's arms

description: A white field bearing the giglio fiorentino (Florentine lily), a stylised red fleur-de-lis, at the centre.
history: The red fleur-de-lis on white has been the emblem of Florence since the 11th century; the colours were reversed (from gold-on-blue to red-on-white) in the 13th century after the Guelph faction took control of the city.

features.symbolism: the historic emblem of Florence; the colours were reversed in the 13th century after the Guelph faction took control of the city
